judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with comparable substance; Report 1 adds extra ground-truth depth (a currently-red test) but neither is materially better on the graded criteria.

report_1.evidence: **Batch B — Registry** (`pipeline/registry.py`, 11 lines): F-02, F-05, F-09. ... I'd give all three to **one** engineer rather than three, since splitting an 11-line file three ways only creates review/merge overhead with no parallelism benefit

report_2.evidence: **Group C — `pipeline/registry.py`, one engineer, but low-risk enough to parallelize internally:** F-02 ..., F-05 ..., F-09 .... I'd still hand this to a single engineer as one small PR rather than three engineers

report_1.evidence: all of A–D can run in the same wave, in parallel ... **Batch C — Rubric parsing** (`pipeline/rubric.py`): F-01 ... paired with F-11 (add the missing edge-case test for rubric parsing). Same engineer ... [Batch B registry] I'd give all three to **one** engineer

report_2.evidence: Group A first ..., Groups B, C, D in parallel alongside or right after A ... **Group B — `pipeline/rubric.py`, one engineer:** F-01 ... followed immediately by F-11 ... **Group C — `pipeline/registry.py`, one engineer** ... F-02, F-05, F-09

report_1.evidence: **F-06** (tighten `PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS`): the finding names no target values ... this needs a rubric/product owner to supply actual numbers before any code changes ... I'd escalate this one and pull it out of the wave rather than assign it.

report_2.evidence: Handing this to an engineer as-is means they'll pick numbers themselves, which is a grading-policy decision (it changes which saved answers get partial credit) ... I'd get explicit target thresholds ... from whoever owns the rubric before assigning implementation.

report_1.evidence: `tests/conftest.py:4` defines `RESULTS_DB = os.environ.get("RESULTS_DB", "/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db")` — a single hardcoded filesystem path with no per-worktree isolation ... running pytest concurrently in separate worktrees on the same machine, they'd collide on that shared path ... an overridden `RESULTS_DB` env var pointing at a per-engineer/per-worktree scratch path, instead of the shared default

report_2.evidence: `tests/conftest.py` defines `RESULTS_DB` defaulting to `/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db`, a path outside the repo ... engineers running tests concurrently on the same machine/CI runner without overriding `RESULTS_DB` per-engineer could collide on that file ... they should export a per-engineer `RESULTS_DB` env var pointing at a local/temp path rather than relying on the shared default
